name: Write a bash script with error handling
task: Create /tmp/eval_backup.sh that takes a source directory and backup directory as arguments, creates a timestamped tar.gz backup of the source into the backup dir (e.g., backup-20260427-141500.tar.gz), and prints the path of the created archive. It should exit 1 with an error message if the source doesn't exist or no arguments are given.

validation.script: set -e; rm -rf /tmp/eval_src /tmp/eval_bak; mkdir -p /tmp/eval_src /tmp/eval_bak; printf 'test\n' > /tmp/eval_src/file.txt; [ -f /tmp/eval_backup.sh ] || { echo 'backup.sh not created'; exit 1; }; OUT=$(bash /tmp/eval_backup.sh /tmp/eval_src /tmp/eval_bak) || { echo 'backup run failed on valid input'; exit 1; }; ARCH=$(ls /tmp/eval_bak/backup-*.tar.gz 2>/dev/null | head -1); [ -n "$ARCH" ] || { echo 'no backup-*.tar.gz archive created'; exit 1; }; case "$OUT" in *backup-*.tar.gz*) : ;; *) echo 'script did not print the archive path'; exit 1;; esac; tar tzf "$ARCH" 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'file.txt' || { echo 'archive does not contain the source file'; exit 1; }; RC=0; bash /tmp/eval_backup.sh /nonexistent_src /tmp/eval_bak >/dev/null 2>&1 || RC=$?; [ "$RC" -eq 1 ] || { echo "nonexistent-source exit got $RC want 1"; exit 1; }; RC=0; bash /tmp/eval_backup.sh >/dev/null 2>&1 || RC=$?; [ "$RC" -eq 1 ] || { echo "no-args exit got $RC want 1"; exit 1; }; echo OK
